name: ai-stepflow
description: Orchestrate Claude agents, skills & workflows for structured AI development.

ai-stepflow.astGraph.enabled.description: Index the workspace with ast-graph and expose it to Claude as an MCP server, so step runs answer structural code questions with a cheap graph query instead of grep+read.

ai-stepflow.astGraph.autoRescanDebounceS.description: How long to wait after a source file save before re-indexing the AST graph.

ai-stepflow.astGraph.binaryPath.description: Absolute path to an ast-graph executable to use instead of downloading the bundled build. Set this on platforms with no prebuilt binary (e.g. Linux arm64) after installing ast-graph yourself. When set, the download and checksum verification are skipped.

ai-stepflow.run.timeoutSeconds.description: Kill a headless step/AI-review run that exceeds this many seconds (it is marked failed). Set to 0 to disable the timeout.

ai-stepflow.run.maxTurns.description: Maximum number of agentic turns per headless step run. Prevents runaway multi-turn execution from consuming excessive tokens. Set to 0 to disable the limit. Individual agents can override this via their maxTurns field.

ai-stepflow.run.headlessMcpServers.description: MCP servers (by name, as configured in your ~/.claude.json) to make available to headless step runs and AI reviews. Default is empty: headless runs carry NO MCP servers, which keeps their tool/instruction context — and token cost — minimal. Add names (e.g. "ast-graph") only for servers a headless step genuinely needs. Does not affect interactive terminal steps, which always use your full MCP config.

walkthroughs.title: Get started with AI StepFlow
walkthroughs.description: Run your Claude agents, skills, and multi-step flows without leaving VS Code.

steps.title: Install the Claude Code CLI
steps.description:
AI StepFlow drives the local `claude` CLI. Install it globally and make sure `claude` is on your PATH:
```
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

steps.title: Install default agents & skills
steps.description:
Add the bundled SDLC agents and skills (plus the project's engineering rules) to your global `~/.claude`.
[Install Default Agents & Skills](command:ai-stepflow.installDefaults)

steps.title: Open the cockpit
steps.description:
Browse global and project agents, skills, and flows side by side.
[Open Cockpit](command:ai-stepflow.openOverview)

media.altText: The AI StepFlow cockpit showing the Workflows board

steps.title: Build and run your first flow
steps.description: Press **+ New Flow**, add steps (assign an agent + skills to each), then press **Run**. Each step opens in a Claude terminal — review the output and approve the gate.
